summary: Mechanical naming cleanup replacing the project term "Action State" with "Motion State" across fighter and item code. The PR renamed core fighter state types, flags, comments, extern declarations, table element types, and local/field identifiers such as asid to msid while preserving existing behavior and table names like MotionStateTable.
what_changed: Renamed the forward-declared fighter state struct from ActionState to MotionState in src/melee/ft/forward.h.
Renamed struct ActionState to struct MotionState in src/melee/ft/types.h.
Updated Fighter fields x1C_actionStateList and x20_actionStateList from ActionState* to MotionState*.
Updated fighter state tables and externs from ActionState to MotionState, including MotionStateList, MotionStateTableByCharacter, and many per-character MotionStateTable arrays.
Renamed Fighter_ActionStateChangeFlags to Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags while leaving the FtStateChange_* flag constants intact.
Renamed many local variables representing state IDs from asid to msid, including calls into Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Applied the same naming direction to item code: ItemStateTable.asid and Item.asid became msid, and Item_80268E5C now takes enum_t msid.
Updated comments and documentation phrases from Action State handler / OnActionStateChange to Motion State handler / OnMotionStateChange.
The PR body records the mechanical commands used: melee_sed 's/Action\( \?State\)/Motion\1/g' and melee_sed 's/\basid\b/msid/g'.
smart_moves: Used a project-aware mechanical replacement, melee_sed, which made a large cross-tree terminology migration consistent across 114 changed files.
Kept semantic table names that already used MotionStateTable, changing only the incorrect ActionState element type to MotionState.
Renamed both type-level terminology and identifier-level shorthand, avoiding a mixed vocabulary where MotionState APIs still used asid locals.
Included comments and Doxygen-style text in the rename, improving searchability and reducing future confusion for decomp contributors.
Did not alter constants such as AS_FOX_SPECIALS_START or APIs like Fighter_ChangeMotionState beyond parameter/local naming, limiting this PR to terminology.
decomp_lessons: For project-wide vocabulary corrections, update core typedefs/structs, forward declarations, table declarations, locals, fields, and comments together so future searches are reliable.
When the existing API name is Fighter_ChangeMotionState and data tables are named MotionStateTable, the underlying type should also be MotionState rather than ActionState.
Mechanical renames can be safe and reviewable when additions and deletions balance and there is no behavioral diff; this PR was 377 additions and 377 deletions.
Preserve established enum constant prefixes when they are outside the intended rename scope; this PR did not rename AS_* constants despite replacing asid locals with msid.
Item state naming should track the same motion-state vocabulary when item state tables and item current state IDs are analogous to fighter motion states.
naming_conventions: Use MotionState, not ActionState, for fighter motion state table element types.
Use Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags, not Fighter_ActionStateChangeFlags, for flags passed to motion-state changes.
Use msid for local variables and fields representing a motion state ID.
Avoid asid for new code when the value is used as a motion-state index or current motion state.
Use "Motion State handler" in comments instead of "Action State handler".
Use "OnMotionStateChange" in comments for callbacks conceptually tied to motion-state changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No matching-specific algorithmic tactic is evidenced; this was a terminology-only mechanical rename.
The rename preserved function signatures and constants where possible, which should avoid codegen changes except for debug/source identifiers and comments.
The PR body documents repeatable sed commands, useful for auditing or replaying equivalent tree-wide naming migrations.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining ActionState or "Action State" references after PR 778.
Search for remaining asid identifiers that may still represent motion state IDs.
Check whether AS_* enum constant naming is intentionally retained or should be addressed separately.
Audit item state comments for consistency with msid and motion-state terminology.
